title: Play Train Table Trundle
summary: A rolling plywood tray sized to slide under a bed, built to hold a train mat and its track — four boards, a plywood bottom, and casters.
description:
About as simple as a rolling storage box gets: a shallow 1x4 frame, a plywood bottom, and four small casters. The only real decision is sizing — this cut list is built around one specific train mat's dimensions, so measure whatever mat or play surface you're actually storing before cutting, rather than trusting the numbers verbatim.

There's nothing here that needs to be strong in the structural sense; the box only ever has to support a folded mat and some track pieces, not weight-bearing loads. Spend your care on getting the casters mounted flat and square instead, since a tray that doesn't roll straight is the one thing that'll actually annoy someone sliding it in and out from under a bed every day.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", cut to 39" x 28-1/2"
materials.note: Ask the store to cut it to size, or resize to fit your own train mat — this cut list matches one specific mat.

cutList.note: Resize to match your actual train mat if it differs.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Join the four 1x4 frame boards at the corners with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, forming a shallow open box.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the plywood bottom to the underside of the frame with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Attach the casters
steps.body: Attach the four caster wheels to the corners of the bottom, making sure they sit flat and square so the tray rolls straight in and out from under the bed.

images.alt: Rolling underbed train table storage tray
